semanticCore: Dust clouds raised in battle, soaking, steeping, stagnant water pool, or loud screaming

keyDerivations.meaning: Dust clouds raised in dry ground by fast running horses, or soaking

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَأَثَرْنَ بِهِ نَقْعًا (100:4): And raising thereby clouds of dust (during the tactical charge of war horses).
discardedNoise: Naq'a (steeping tea bags in hot water) - modern culinary.
summaryEn: The root represents dust clouds stirred by high-speed motion (Naq'), capturing the intense battle charge of horses whose hooves raise dust columns in the morning air.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الغبار الثائر المنتشر في الجو بفعل حوافر الخيل السريعة، ونقع السوائل (نقع)، واصفاً إثارة خيل الغزاة للغبار الكثيف في هجومهم الصباحي.
rawText: أَنْقَعَ 4 see 1. أَنْقَعُ More, or most, thirst-quenching: see an ex. under الحَاذُ, in art. حوذ; and another voce رَشَفَ. أَنْقُوعَةٌ The hollow, or depression, of ثَرِيد, (S, A, K,) in which the gravy collects. (A, K. *) إِسْتَنْقَعَ 10 see 1. سَمٌّ نَاقِعٌ Poison that takes effect; (S, K;) that kills: (TA:) that remains fixed, (Abu-nNasr, K, TA,) and collects. (Abu-Nasr, TA.) مَسْتَنْقَعٌ : see مَنْقَعٌ. مَنْقَعُ البُرْمِ Untwisted old thread which a woman spins a second time, and puts into the stone cooking-pots, because she has nothing but these [in which to deposit it]. (Sgh, K, TA. [From the K it would seem to be مُنْقعٌ alone: and in the CK, البُرام is erroneously put for البِرام: Golius found it written البَرَام; and has wrongly explained it in his Appendix.]) === سَمٌّ مُنْقَعٌ Poison made into a confection. (S, K, TA.) مَنْقَعُ مَآءِ , and 🡻مَسْتَنْقَعُمَآءٍ, A place where water remains and collects; where it collects and stagnates; or where it remains long, and becomes altered. See نَقَعَ. نَقَعَ 1 and 🡻اِسْتَنْقَعَ It (water) remained, or stagnated, or collected, in a hollow, or cavity: (Mgh:) or remained long, and became altered: (Msb:) or the former [and latter] collected in a مَنْقَع: (S:) or the latter [and former] became yellow and altered. (K.) === نَقَعَ and 🡻أَنْقَعَ [He macerated, steeped, or soaked, a medicine, in water,] he left it in water until its colour became changed. (Msb.) نَقِيعٌ An infusion; meaning, a beverage made by steeping something in water: (Msb:) and a mash.
